title: Cell potency assays, platforms, and methods of use
abstract: A high-throughput, scalable, low-cost, on-chip microfluidic potency assay for nondifferentiated cells such as human mesenchymal stromal cells (hMSCs) with improved functional predictive power and recapitulation of in vivo secretory responses compared to traditional approaches. By comparison of hMSC secretory responses to functional hMSC- medicated immune cell suppression, on-chip microfluidic potency markers are identified with improved functional predictive power compared to traditional planar methods.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A microfluidic potency assay comprising living cells, wherein the cells are encapsulated in a synthetic hydrogel, and further wherein the hydrogel is incorporated into a closed system which is perfused with liquid media and/or disease-relevant chemical stimuli. 2. The assay of claim 1, wherein the cells are differentiating cells. 3. The assay of claim 2 , wherein the differentiating cells are human mesenchymal stromal cells. 4. The assay of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the synthetic hydrogel comprises 4-arm maleimide-functionalized poly(ethylene-glycol) (PEG-4MAL). 5. The assay of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the hydrogel is cross-linked with a protease-degradable peptide. 6. The assay of claim 5, wherein the peptide comprises VPM. 7. The assay of claim 6, wherein the peptide comprising VPM is GCRDVPMSMRGGDRCG (SEQ ID NO: 1). 8. The assay of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the hydrogel is cross-linked with a dithiolated molecule. 9. The assay of claim 8, wherein the dithiolated molecule comprises dithiothreitol (DTT). 10. The assay of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the hydrogel comprises an adhesive peptide. 11. The assay of claim 10, wherein the adhesive peptide comprises RGD. 12. The assay of claim 11, wherein the adhesive peptide comprising RGD is GRGDSPC (SEQ ID NO: 2). 13. The assay of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the liquid media comprises IFN-y or other chemical stimuli relevant to disease indication. 14. The assay of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the closed system comprised of silicone polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS). 15. A platform comprising the assay of any one of claims 1-14. 16. The platform of claim 15, wherein the platform is high-throughput. 17. The platform of claim 15 or 16, wherein the platform is scalable. 18. A method of assessing viability, health, or in vivo secretion of a cell culture, the method comprising: a. providing a microfluidic potency assay comprising living cells, wherein the cells are encapsulated in a polyethylene glycol (PEG) hydrogel, and further wherein the hydrogel is incorporated into a closed system which is perfused with liquid media and/or disease-relevant chemical stimuli; b. obtaining one or more liquid samples from the cell culture; and c. detecting one or more markers in the sample or samples obtained from the cell culture; d. analyzing said markers to assess the viability, health, or in vivo secretion of the cell culture. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the marker is an analyte produced by the cell. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the analyte is part of the autocrine or paracrine system. 21. The method of any one of claims 18-20, wherein the analyte is secreted protein(s), cytokine, chemokine, enzyme, metabolite, or other cell product(s). 22. The method of any one of claims 18-21, wherein during the step of analyzing, secretory response of the cell culture is analyzed. 23. The method of claim 20, wherein the cellâs secretome is analyzed. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein said secretome is analyzed in a way that doesnât damage the cells. 25. The method of any one of claims 18-24, wherein metrics of secretion of the cell culture relevant to its physiological environment are measured. 26. The method of any one of claims 18-25, wherein the cell performance is compared to hydrogel in vivo. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the in vivo cell culture is used for assay creation, optimization, and/or validation. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the in vivo model introduces a cell-laden hydrogel polymerized in situ of the subcutaneous space of a subject. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the subject is an animal. 30. The method of any one of claims 18-29, wherein cell culture in vivo performance is detected by detecting at least one analyte, wherein at least one analyte is MMP-13.
